steps.title: Identify the turning point
steps.content:
The author uses “not inconceivable” to acknowledge the late-dating theory is plausible, then pivots with “but”: advocates have not justified why contextual fit should override linguistic evidence pointing to an eighth- or seventh-century composition.

The passage is not questioning the historical conditions—it is challenging the prioritization of contextual fit over linguistic evidence.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content:
D directly matches the passage's argument structure.
  • A mischaracterizes the issue as evidence being “called into question”
  • B redirects the problem to “controversial interpretation” of conditions
  • C adds an unsupported symmetric-fit claim
. That matches choice D — D is correct.
